name: Fast Cash Funding
description_short: Fast Cash Funding offers merchant cash advances and cash flow credit programs to small businesses, with same-day approval available. They provide up to $1M in funding for inventory, equipment, payroll, and growth.
description_long:
Fast Cash Funding is a business funding provider based in Miami, Florida, operating from the Citigroup Center at 201 S Biscayne Blvd. The company positions itself as a flexible alternative lender for small business owners who may face barriers with traditional lenders, explicitly stating they work with businesses "no matter your credit." They market aggressively on speed and accessibility, prominently featuring same-day approval and funding capabilities.

The company offers two primary products: Merchant Cash Advances (MCA) for businesses accepting credit or debit cards, and a Cash Flow Credit Program that provides yearly funding disbursed monthly to cover expenses like payroll and inventory. They claim to provide up to $1 million in funding and position their funding specialists as consultants who present multiple options rather than pushing a single product.

What distinguishes Fast Cash Funding is their explicit acceptance of lower credit profiles and emphasis on speed—same-day approval is prominently advertised on their homepage. They frame themselves as advisors helping businesses choose rather than gatekeepers, and their testimonials emphasize approval despite previous rejections from other lenders. The company operates with a relatively minimal web presence; several subpages appear to have 404 errors or placeholder content, suggesting a lightweight digital footprint.

Fast Cash Funding should be evaluated with caution as a traditional funding source. Merchant cash advances are not loans and typically carry significantly higher effective cost rates than conventional small business loans. The website lacks critical transparency around pricing, APR, fees, repayment terms, and actual approval requirements despite claims of flexibility. The aggressive "same-day approval" marketing and emphasis on serving rejected applicants suggests a predatory lending model. No regulatory disclosures, licensing information, or third-party reviews are presented on the website.
pros: Advertises same-day approval and funding availability, appealing to businesses needing urgent capital
Explicitly states willingness to work with businesses regardless of credit profile
Offers two distinct product types (MCA and cash flow programs) to address different business needs
Provides up to $1 million in funding amounts suitable for genuine small business growth
Monthly disbursement option for cash flow programs rather than lump-sum pressure
No minimum industry restrictions claimed—serves businesses in any industry
cons: Merchant cash advances typically carry far higher effective costs than traditional loans, often 40-200%+ APR equivalent, with no pricing transparency on website
Critical information absent: no APR, fees, repayment terms, or actual approval criteria disclosed despite claims of flexibility
Minimal website substance with multiple 404 errors and lack of detailed product documentation raises credibility concerns
No regulatory licensing, certifications, or third-party reviews provided; no Better Business Bureau or customer rating visibility
Targeting of rejected applicants and aggressive speed marketing are typical predatory lending indicators
best_for: Small business owners who accept credit cards and need quick capital injection despite poor traditional lending options
Merchants with strong credit card sales who can sustain factor-rate repayments from future receipts
Business owners willing to pay premium costs in exchange for genuinely fast approval and minimal documentation
diagnosis: Fast Cash Funding is best for small business owners accepting credit cards who have been rejected by traditional lenders and need capital immediately, but recognize the critical caveat that merchant cash advances are expensive non-loan products with effective costs potentially exceeding 100% APR. Business owners should only pursue this after exhausting conventional SBA loans, bank lines of credit, and CDFI lenders, as the long-term cost of capital here is substantial.
services: Merchant Cash Advance (MCA) - funding against future credit/debit card sales
Cash Flow Credit Program - yearly funding disbursed monthly for payroll and inventory
